figures.caption: Figure 7: Per-round metrics curve of no-op and cycle editing. On average, VAE-LFA improves visual consistency both from a macro (LPIPS) and micro (SSIM) perspective.
Figure 2: Cross-architecture validation of frequency-wise drift. Relative radial-spectrum difference between DiT-only and VAE-only loops over 10 rounds: ∆P (r) = PDiT(r)−PVAE(r)
Table 1: No-op and cycle editing results for all models. We abbreviate Qwen Image 2.0 as Qwen and Seedream 4.0 as Seedream. The better result between LFA/non-LFA variants is shown in bold.
Table 2: Long-chain editing results for all models.
Figure 3: Overview of VAE-LFA: component-wise drift analysis and low-frequency latent alignment.

Figure 12: Visualizations of black-box long-chain edits. On Seedream 4.0, VAE-LFA improves image consistency and instruction following capabilities considerably, while on Qwen Image 2.0, VAE-LFA delays image quality degradation significantly.
Figure 8: Visualization of No-op editing trajectories at round 10.
Figure 9: Visualization of Cycle editing results at round 10.
Figure 10: Visualization of white-box long-chain edits at round 2, 4, 6, 8, 10.
Figure 4: Low/high-frequency components in the FLUX.2 VAE latent space. Low frequencies capture global appearance (e.g., color); high frequencies retain local details (e.g., texture).
Table 7: Ablation studies on black-box models. The best result among variants of each base editor is shown in bold.

Figure 11: Visualization of black-box long-chain edits. On Seedream 4.0, VAE-LFA improves image consistency and instruction following capabilities considerably, while on Qwen Image 2.0, VAE-LFA delays image quality degradation significantly.
Table 4: Ablation on frequency alignment scope for FLUX.2. Best results are in bold. ∗ uses the DiT-only setting; Low only is VAE-LFA.
Table 5: Ablation on anchor update strategy for FLUX.2. EMA is the default VAE-LFA setting.
Figure 5: Low/high-frequency drift between round 1 (up) & 10 (down) under three different settings.
Figure 6: Removing VAE round trips improves high-frequency fidelity but leaves low-frequency drift.
